alerts.name: X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
alerts.desc: The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'. This allows older versions of Internet Explorer and Chrome to perform MIME-sniffing on the response body.

instances.otherinfo: This issue still applies to error type pages (401, 403, 500, etc.) as those pages are often still affected by injection issues.

alerts.solution: Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately, and that it sets the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' for all web pages.

alerts.name: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
alerts.desc: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is an attack technique that involves echoing attacker-supplied code into a user's browser instance.
alerts.solution: Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library or framework that does not allow this weakness to occur.

alerts.name: Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set
alerts.desc: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is a web security policy mechanism whereby a web server declares that complying user agents are to interact with it using only secure HTTPS connections.
alerts.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.

alerts.name: Secure Pages Include Mixed Content (Including Scripts)
alerts.desc: The page includes mixed content, that is content accessed via HTTP instead of HTTPS.

instances.otherinfo: A script was loaded via HTTP: http://cdn.example.com/analytics.js

alerts.solution: A page that is available over SSL/TLS must be comprised completely of content which is transmitted over SSL/TLS.
